oligos.name: Barcoded Bead SeqB (bead capture oligo)
oligos.role: Bead-tethered capture/RT primer: SMART PCR handle + cell barcode + UMI + oligo-dT (recommended go-forward bead; used in retina replicates 4-7)

components.name: Bead-Linker
components.role: 18-carbon PEG flexible-chain linker tethering the oligo to the Toyopearl bead (sequence not specified)

oligos.notes: Table S6. 5'-Bead-Linker-TTTTTTTAAGCAGTGGTATCAACGCAGAGTAC JJJJJJJJJJJJ NNNNNNNN T(30)-3'. Prime Read 1 with Read1CustomSeqB. >10^8 primers per bead.

oligos.name: Barcoded Bead SeqA (bead capture oligo)
oligos.role: Bead-tethered capture/RT primer variant with a longer handle (...GAGTACGT); used in the human-mouse experiments and retina replicates 1-3
oligos.notes: Table S6. 5'-Bead-Linker-TTTTTTTAAGCAGTGGTATCAACGCAGAGTACGT JJJJJJJJJJJJ NNNNNNNN T(30)-3'. Prime Read 1 with Read1CustomSeqA.

oligos.name: Template_Switch_Oligo (TSO)
oligos.role: Template-switching oligo; its 3′ riboG triplet pairs the untemplated C's added by Maxima H- RT, appending the SMART handle to the cDNA 3′ end

components.name: Riboguanosines
components.role: 3′ rGrGrG that base-pairs with cDNA 3′ dC overhang

oligos.notes: Table S6: AAGCAGTGGTATCAACGCAGAGTGAAT rGrGrG (folded rGrGrG -> GGG). Used at 2.5 µM (supp) / 50 µM stock (protocol) in RT.

oligos.name: TSO_PCR / SMART PCR Primer (Template_Switch_PCR primer)
oligos.role: Whole-transcriptome cDNA amplification primer; anneals to the SMART handle present on both cDNA ends after template switching
oligos.notes: Table S6 TSO_PCR. Called 'SMART PCR PRIMER' (100 µM) in the protocol; 0.8 µM in supp.

oligos.name: P5-TSO_Hybrid (New-P5-SMART PCR hybrid oligo)
oligos.role: Library-PCR forward primer after tagmentation; appends the Illumina P5 adapter to the SMART/3′-cDNA end

components.name: SMART handle
components.role: anneals to bead SMART handle; ends with phosphorothioate *A*C

oligos.notes: Table S6 P5-TSO_Hybrid: AATGATACGGCGACCACCGAGATCTACACGCCTGTCCGCGGAAGCAGTGGTATCAACGCAGAGT*A*C (3′ *A*C = phosphorothioate). Used at 200 nM in Nextera PCR.

oligos.name: Nextera_N701 (N70X i7 library primer)
oligos.role: Library-PCR reverse primer after tagmentation; appends the i7 sample index + Illumina P7 adapter to the Nextera (Tn5) end

components.name: Nextera Read 2 handle (s7)
components.role: anneals to Nextera mosaic-end/s7 region installed by tagmentation

oligos.notes: Table S6. Condition-1 index in species-contamination experiment. Used at 200 nM in Nextera PCR.

oligos.name: Nextera_N702 (i7 library primer)
oligos.role: Alternate i7-indexed library-PCR reverse primer (species-contamination condition 2)

oligos.name: Nextera_N703 (i7 library primer)
oligos.role: Alternate i7-indexed library-PCR reverse primer (species-contamination condition 3)

oligos.name: Read1CustomSeqA (custom Read 1 sequencing primer)
oligos.role: Custom Read 1 sequencing primer for libraries made with Barcoded Bead SeqA; primes the cell barcode + UMI read
oligos.notes: Table S6. Loaded at 0.3 µM for Read 1 priming on NextSeq/MiSeq.

oligos.name: Read1CustomSeqB (custom Read 1 sequencing primer)
oligos.role: Custom Read 1 sequencing primer for libraries made with Barcoded Bead SeqB; primes the cell barcode + UMI read

oligos.name: P7-TSO_Hybrid
oligos.role: P7-side hybrid PCR primer used in the bead-primer RT validation (soluble-RNA) experiment to amplify off the SMART handle
oligos.notes: Table S6: CAAGCAGAAGACGGCATACGAGATCGTGATCGGTCTCGGCGGAAGCAGTGGTATCAACGCAGAGT*A*C. Used at 400 nM with TruSeq_F.

oligos.name: TruSeq_F (standard Illumina TruSeq primer)
oligos.role: P5/TruSeq Read 1 primer used in the soluble-RNA validation PCR and to prime Read 1 for the synRNA molecular-barcode read

oligos.name: CustSynRNASeq
oligos.role: Custom Read 2 sequencing primer used in the synRNA bead-barcode-homogeneity experiment to read the 12-bp cell barcode + 8-bp UMI

oligos.name: UMI_SMARTdT
oligos.role: In-solution RT primer (SMART handle + 9-nt UMI + oligo-dT) for the standard in-solution template-switch amplification comparison

oligos.name: synRNA (polyadenylated synthetic RNA spike-in)
oligos.role: Polyadenylated synthetic RNA carrying a 20-nt molecular barcode; used to quantify bead primer sites and validate barcode readout

components.name: TruSeq-derived RNA handle
components.role: RNA sequence homologous to TruSeq Read 1 region (ribo)

oligos.notes: Table S6, given as ribonucleotides (folded rX -> X; RNA U retained). Read 1 (TruSeq primer) reads the 20-bp molecular barcode.

final_library.source_label: Drop-seq final Illumina library (Barcoded Bead SeqB chemistry; P5-TSO_Hybrid + Nextera N701)
annotation_lines: AAGCAGTGGTATCAACGCAGAGTAC = SMART PCR handle (Read 1 primer region)
CTGTCTCTTATACACATCT = Nextera Read 2 mosaic end (revcomp of ME)

evidence.locator: Drop-seq final Illumina library (Barcoded Bead SeqB chemistry; P5-TSO_Hybrid + Nextera N701)

library_generation.title: mRNA capture on barcoded bead (droplet co-encapsulation + lysis)
library_generation.summary: A lysed cell's polyadenylated mRNA hybridizes by its poly(A) tail to the 30-nt oligo-dT of a co-encapsulated barcoded bead.
library_generation.note: The co-flow device pairs one cell with one bead in a ~1 nL droplet; lysis buffer (Ficoll/Sarkosyl/Tris/EDTA/DTT) releases mRNA that anneals to the bead-tethered oligo-dT.

library_generation.title: Reverse transcription with template switching (STAMP formation)
library_generation.summary: Maxima H- RT extends the bead primer into first-strand cDNA and adds untemplated dC's at the 3′ end.
library_generation.note: Reverse transcription copies the mRNA into cDNA covalently attached to the bead (a STAMP); the terminal-transferase activity of Maxima H- appends 2-3 dC's to the cDNA 3′ end.

library_generation.title: Template switch appends SMART handle to cDNA 3′ end
library_generation.summary: The TSO rGrGrG anneals to the cDNA dC overhang and is copied, placing a SMART handle on the cDNA 3′ end.
library_generation.note: Both ends of the first-strand cDNA now carry the AAGCAGTGGTATCAACGCAGAGT SMART handle, enabling single-primer whole-transcriptome PCR.

library_generation.title: Exonuclease I treatment
library_generation.summary: Exonuclease I chews back bead primers that never captured an mRNA, leaving only extended STAMPs.
library_generation.note: Single-stranded, unextended oligos are degraded so they cannot participate in PCR; barcode-bearing extended primers (double-stranded at the 5′ handle after RT) are protected.

library_generation.title: Whole-transcriptome PCR (SMART/TSO_PCR primer)
library_generation.summary: A single SMART PCR primer amplifies full-length cDNA off both handle-bearing ends.
library_generation.note: Kapa HiFi + TSO_PCR/SMART primer (AAGCAGTGGTATCAACGCAGAGT) produce double-stranded, amplified cDNA averaging 1300-2000 bp.

library_generation.title: Nextera XT tagmentation (Tn5)
library_generation.summary: Tn5 fragments the amplified cDNA and inserts the Nextera mosaic-end adapter.
library_generation.note: Tagmentation adds the Nextera Read 2 / s7 handle to internal 3′-biased fragments; only fragments still bearing the 5′ SMART handle can later receive P5, restricting the library to 3′ ends.

library_generation.title: Nextera library PCR (P5-TSO_Hybrid + Nextera N70X)
library_generation.summary: P5-TSO_Hybrid adds P5 at the SMART end and Nextera N70X adds the i7 index + P7 at the tagmented end.
library_generation.note: Because P5 is added only via the SMART handle, exclusively 3′-end cDNA fragments are amplified into the final sequenceable library (~450-680 bp).

library_sequencing.note: Custom Read 1 primer anneals to the bottom strand across the bridge + SMART handle; its 3' end sits at the barcode boundary and extension reads the 12-bp cell barcode (bases 1-12) then the 8-bp UMI (bases 13-20).

library_sequencing.primer: Nextera i7 index sequencing primer (anneals to the s7 handle: 5'-...CCGAGCCCACGAGAC-3'); only when multiplexing
library_sequencing.note: The index primer matches the top-strand s7 handle, so it anneals to the bottom strand with its 3' end at the index boundary and reads the 8-bp i7 sample index installed by the Nextera N70X primer.

The s7 Read 2 primer anneals to the top strand across the Nextera mosaic-end + s7 region; its 3' end points into the insert and extension reads back through the 3' cDNA fragment (50 bp; 60 bp in the retina experiment).

title: Drop-seq
description: Drop-seq is a droplet-based, massively parallel 3′ single-cell RNA-seq method. Individual cells are co-encapsulated with a DNA-barcoded microparticle ("bead") in a nanoliter (~1 nL, 125 µm) aqueous droplet formed by a co-flow microfluidic device; the cell lyses inside the droplet and its polyadenylated mRNAs hybridize to the bead's oligo-dT primers. Each bead carries a common SMART/PCR handle, a 12-bp split-pool cell barcode (one of 4^12 = 16,777,216), an 8-bp UMI, and a 30-bp oligo-dT. After bulk reverse transcription with template switching (STAMP formation), exonuclease treatment, whole-transcriptome PCR, and Nextera XT tagmentation of the 3′ ends, the library is sequenced on Illumina: Read 1 (20 bp) yields the cell barcode + UMI, and Read 2 the cDNA.

original_publication.title: Highly Parallel Genome-wide Expression Profiling of Individual Cells Using Nanoliter Droplets

throughput.summary: ~10,000 single-cell libraries prepared per hour at 100 cells/µL (12 hr for 10,000 cells); ~1,200/hr at 12.5 cells/µL. ~6.5 cents per cell. 44,808 mouse retinal cells profiled into 39 clusters.
throughput.cells: 10,000 single-cell libraries in ~12 hr; 44,808 retinal cells analyzed (49,300 STAMPs sequenced)
throughput.rna: At saturating depth ~44,295 transcripts / 6,722 genes per HEK cell and ~26,044 transcripts / 5,663 genes per 3T3 cell; ~12.8% transcript capture efficiency (ERCC); ~10.7% by ddPCR
throughput.dna: Amplified cDNA ~1300-2000 bp; final tagmented library ~450-680 bp; yield 10-30 nM (HEK/3T3)

publication.statistical_model: Digital transcript counting via UMIs with edit-distance-1 (substitution-only recommended) collapse; single-cell purity and doublet rates estimated from species-mixing (binomial co-encapsulation) experiments.

other.label: Droplet size
other.value: ~125 µm diameter, ~1 nL, generated on a PDMS co-flow device (>100,000 droplets/min)

other.label: Bead synthesis
other.value: Reverse-direction (5'->3') phosphoramidite synthesis on Toyopearl HW-65S resin by Chemgenes

other.label: Capture efficiency
other.value: ~12.8% (ERCC spike-in), corroborated at ~10.7% by droplet digital PCR

other.label: Protocol source
other.value: Drop-seq Laboratory Protocol v1.1 (Aug 6, 2015), Macosko & Goldman, McCarroll Lab (www.mccarrolllab.com/dropseq)

data_processing.summary: Read 1 supplies the 12-bp cell barcode + 8-bp UMI, which are tagged onto the paired cDNA read; the cDNA (Read 2) is aligned to the reference genome, reads are grouped by cell barcode, and UMIs are collapsed per gene to give digital transcript counts — producing a cell-by-gene digital gene-expression (DGE) matrix that is then clustered. The paper and protocol point to the McCarroll-lab 'Drop-seq tools' software (Nemesh, with help from Wysoker) and an accompanying downloadable informatics guide.
data_processing.statistical_model: Digital transcript counting via UMIs collapsed at edit distance 1 (substitution-only); single-cell purity and doublet rate estimated from species-mixing (binomial co-encapsulation) analysis.
